REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. NON-OPPOSITION FILED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUIET ENJOYMENT FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE A CONTRACT-BASED (AS OPPOSED TO TORT-BASED) CAUSE OF ACTION AND SEEK CORRESPONDING DAMAGES AS TO THE UNIT SHE CURRENTLY OCCUPIES (432) AS ASSERTED IN OPPOSITION. DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION OF ACTION FOR UCL VIOLATION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS CONSTITUTING UNFAIR, UNLAWFUL, OR FRAUDULENT PRACTICES WITHIN THE MEANING OF UCL AND WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO TO ALLEGE A REMEDY AVAILABLE UNDER THIS STATUTE. DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES A BREACH AND AN EXCUSE FOR HER OWN NON-PERFORMANCE UNDER THE CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTACT CLAUSES FOR LANDLORD TO PROVIDE POSSESSION OF THE NEW UNIT AND THE TENANT TO SURRENDER POSSESSION OF THE OLD UNIT AS DEPENDENT CLAUSES IS PLAUSIBLE AND MUST BE ACCEPTED AS TRUE ON A DEMURRER. DEMURRER BASED ON MISJOINDER IS OVERRULED. SECTION 23 OF THE LEASE DOES NOT RELEASE THE FORMER OWNER FROM LIABILITY FOR THE WRONGS/BREACHES COMMITTED PRIOR TO THE TRANSFER. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT OFFERS TO PREPARE THE ORDER. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

John P. Byrne; The ByrneLaw Office; 818-593-5520; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Stonestown Shopping Center, L.P.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANGELA LEUNG'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING MOTION OF DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER LP FOR COSTS AND ATTORNEYS FEES is denied. Although Ms. Leung fails to show any new facts, law or circumstances as required by CCP 1008, the court, on its own motion per its inherent authority, once again considered whether paragraph 20.2 of the parties' lease provided for an award of fees to SSC. The court remains of the view that paragraph 20.2 is a prevailing party fees provision that is applicable to the circumstances of this case. SSC's request for supplemental fees is granted in part. SSC is awarded $490 in fees based on two hours of its counsel's time. Given the simplicity of this motion, the reasonable amount of time needed to oppose the motion does not exceed two hours.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Stonestown Shopping Center, Lp For An Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of John P. Byrne
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

WookSun Hong; Law Offices of WookSun Hong/Bay Area Legal Incubator; 510-368-8538; appearing in person on behalf of plaintiff Angela Leung.
John P. Byrn; The ByrneLaw Office; 818-593-5520; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Stonestown Shopping Center, L.P.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Defendant contests the portion of the tentative ruling that states that the fees should be apportioned.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LP Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Stonestown Shopping Center, LP For An Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs is granted in large part. The Court assumes that this motion is still in dispute notwithstanding the filing of a notice of settlement of the entire case on June 9, 2021 because the parties filed supplemental papers on this motion after that date. Based on that assumption, SSC is awarded $34,838.81 in attorneys' fees and $2,327.31 in costs. Section 20.2 of the parties' lease includes a fees and costs provision which provides for the award of fees and costs to the prevailing party in this case. SSC is the prevailing party. The fees and costs sought by SSC are adequately documented and the hourly rates and number of hours are reasonable. However, section 20.2 only covers contract claims. After a review of the court file, the court concludes that the fees should be apportioned two-thirds to the contract and one-third to the non-contract claims. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.
Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Non-opposition filed.
Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for Quiet Enjoyment for Plaintiff to allege a contract-based (as opposed to tort-based) cause of action and seek corresponding damages as to the unit she currently occupies (432) as asserted in opposition. Demurrer to cause of action of action for UCL violation is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts constituting unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent practices within the meaning of UCL and with requisite specificity. Plaintiff is also to allege a remedy available under this statute.
Demurrer to causes of action for breach of contract and covenant of good faith and fair dealing is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a breach and an excuse for her own non-performance under the contract. Plaintiff's interpretation of the contact clauses for landlord to provide possession of the new unit and the tenant to surrender possession of the old unit as dependent clauses is plausible and must be accepted as true on a demurrer.
Demurrer based on misjoinder is overruled. Section 23 of the Lease does not release the former owner from liability for the wrongs/breaches committed prior to the transfer. Counsel for the Defendant offers to prepare the order. Defendant to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
